summary: Iteration 5 (Life Events) — end-to-end PASS on both backend and frontend. Backend: 28/28 pytest pass (21 baseline regression + 7 new event tests). Verified: events list persists on POST /api/members with provided or auto-generated ids; PATCH /api/members/{id} fully replaces the events array; events:[] clears the list; invalid event 'type' is rejected with 422; events default to [] on get; non-event fields (bio, birth_date, marriages, partner_ids) preserved when only events patched. Frontend: rendered all 6 seeded members; opened TEST_ParentA Details panel — 'LIFE EVENTS' section shows 4 preloaded events (Graduated Yale 1938 New Haven, Married Eleanor 1948, Founded Whitfield & Co. 1955 Boston, Moved to NYC 1970 New York) with the correct phosphor icons (GraduationCap/HeartStraight/Briefcase/Airplane) and gold-colored years. Clicked 'add-event-button' → inline form revealed with all expected fields (event-type-select, event-year-input, event-title-input, event-location-input, save-event-button, cancel-event-button). Selected Career, filled 1985 / TestEventAuto / TestCity, clicked Save → row count 4→5, API confirmed parentA.events=5 and total=8 (was 7). Switched to Timeline mode (button text correctly toggles Timeline→Tree). Rewind → 0 badges. Forward → 8 event-badge-* elements (matches API total events across all members). Each badge has the 'event-badge-pop' class and 5 distinct inline style colors (one per type/event). Switched back to Tree mode → 0 badges. Deleted TestEventAuto via the delete-event-{id} hover button → row count 5→4. Regression: search-input, add-member-button, mode-toggle-button, and timeline scrubber all present and functional.
critical_code_review_comments: EventsSection.handleAdd uses crypto.randomUUID() — already guarded with a fallback. Note backend also assigns a uuid when id is missing, so an alternative is to omit the client-side id entirely and trust the server reply. Not a bug.

MemberNode badge stack is positioned with translateX(100%) right of the avatar; with 8+ events on one member the stack could grow beyond Drei <Html> bounds. With current seed (max 4 per member) this is fine. Consider a max-height with vertical scroll or a 'group +N' indicator if a member ever accumulates >6 events.

Server-side LifeEvent model uses Literal[...] for type — invalid types correctly produce 422. No additional bounds on year (e.g., < 0 or > 3000) — likely fine, but worth a UX hint in the form if abuse becomes a concern.

PATCH replaces the full events array. This is documented and matches the frontend behavior (add and delete both PATCH a fresh full list). No issue, just noting it's destructive if two clients edit concurrently — acceptable for a single-user app.
seed_data_creation: Recreated 5 TEST_ members with events via /tmp/seed_events.py: TEST_ParentA (b.1920 d.1995, 4 events: education 1938, marriage 1948, career 1955, migration 1970), TEST_ParentB (b.1925 d.2002, partner of A, marriage 1948; 1 event: migration 1972), TEST_ChildC (b.1950, child of A+B, 1 event: education 1972), TEST_FullE (b.1955, child of A+B, 0 events), TEST_HalfD (b.1965, child of A only, 1 event: other 1990). Krishna (b.1921 d.2021) preserved with 0 events (cleared the TestEventAuto leftover from earlier UI run). Total = 6 members, 7 base events (8 after the in-test 'TestEventAuto' add, then 7 again after delete cleanup at end of test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 (Life Events) is fully working. All acceptance criteria met. 6 members are seeded (5 TEST_ + 1 Krishna). DB total events at end-of-run = 7 (matches what was seeded). pytest cleanup fixtures in test_events_api.py only delete TEST_EVT_-prefixed members, so they will NOT touch the iteration seed; however test_members_api.py and test_new_features.py cleanup fixtures DO delete anything starting with 'TEST_' on module-teardown — so running the full backend pytest suite WILL wipe the iteration seed. Reseed via /tmp/seed_events.py if you need a populated tree for visual verification. timeline-forward jumps to year=max so document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid^="event-badge-"]').length should equal sum of events across all members at that point.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Note for self: my first pass through pytest wiped the iteration seed (test_members_api.py module fixture deletes all TEST_ prefixed members on teardown). Reseed required before UI testing — handled and noted in context for next agent.
